summary: Excellent soundboard recording with high‑energy playing – Sugaree, Friend of the Devil and a smoking Dark Star stand out.
review: The show bursts with heat from the opening up‑tempo Sugaree through tight harmonies on Friend of the Devil and rolls into a cosmic second set highlighted by a stellar Dark Star. The soundboard source is praised as great and clear, making this Miami/Orlando late‑’89 run a must‑listen for fans.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviewers are enthusiastic; three give 5 stars and one gives 4, noting superb playing and clear soundboard quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the sound "great" and "crisp"; no drop‑outs or balance issues are mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A high‑octane opening four songs set a frantic pace that never eases, leading into a tight, cosmic second set with nonstop momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is repeatedly praised for swinging guitar work, note‑bending solos and "killing" the performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s keyboards and high‑in‑mix vocals are highlighted as a major lift; his playing on Little Light is called "smoking."
